inputRoute: Built-in MacBook Air microphone at 44100 Hz; no wired, Bluetooth, or external route tested
viewport: Desktop Safari, 800 CSS px reported by WebDriver; 200 percent zoom and physical mobile layouts not established
assistiveTechnology: Safari WebDriver DOM inspection only; VoiceOver was not used
thermalObservation: No operating-system thermal warning was observed during the short session; battery change includes unrelated development and browser activity and is not attributed to Live Staff

layout.notes: Safari reported an 800 CSS px desktop viewport with no document-level horizontal overflow. Direct visual inspection at 200 percent zoom, 320 CSS px, portrait, and landscape was not completed.

permission-start.notes: A trusted Safari WebDriver click on Start caused the only microphone prompt. The operator selected Allow. The app then displayed Listening locally at 44100 Hz and live pitch content began updating.

pitch-history.notes: The current staff text, current note, and bounded 10-second history updated continuously from the built-in microphone. The source was uncontrolled room input rather than a named voice, instrument, or generated acoustic tone, so pitch accuracy is not claimed and this row remains blocked.

transposition.notes: Changing from concert pitch to B-flat trumpet immediately changed the same live input presentation from concert E3 to written G-flat3 and identified Written pitch for B-flat trumpet in text and the grand-staff label. Because the uncontrolled source was not independently known, this demonstrates immediate written-pitch presentation but does not validate note accuracy.

calibration.notes: Calibration completed during active capture and reported Room noise calibrated locally for this listening session. Listening continued afterward. The session-only wording was present; no detector-accuracy conclusion is drawn.

filters.notes: One band-stop filter was added with named enabled, type, frequency, width, reduction, remove, bypass, and reset controls. Bypass was toggled and the monitor reported that pitch detection received raw input while bypassed. Filter creation required a scripted click after WebDriver element clicks did not activate it, and no named source supported an audible enabled-versus-bypassed comparison, so keyboard operability and effect remain blocked.

monitor.notes: The opt-in monitor was enabled during active capture and displayed two local canvases, waveform and raw spectrum/filter response text, and a live level. Disabling it removed both canvases immediately while listening remained active.

stop.notes: Stop changed the app to Listening stopped. Microphone resources were released and restored Start listening. The operator could not attend the macOS microphone indicator, so operating-system track release is not independently passed.

background.notes: Switching to Chrome while Safari captured changed Safari to Listening paused while the page was in the background. Resume listening to continue. Returning Safari to the foreground and activating Resume restored Listening locally at 44100 Hz without another permission prompt.

screen-lock.notes: The Mac was not locked during active capture. Operator step: start Safari listening, lock and unlock macOS, record the resulting status, then perform the single offered recovery action.

interruption.notes: No controllable operating-system audio interruption was performed. Operator step: cause an available audio-session interruption during capture and record state and recovery without inferring phone behavior from desktop.

permission-revocation.notes: Permission was legitimately granted, but it was not revoked during or between sessions. Operator step: revoke this site's microphone permission in Safari settings, confirm the app guidance and inactive indicator, then restore only through normal browser UI.

route-change.notes: Only the built-in microphone was available. No wired, Bluetooth, external-input removal, or active route-switch claim is made.

keyboard.notes: WebDriver key input reached the primary button and native names, values, disabled states, and disclosure states were present in DOM inspection. Direct visible-focus inspection, complete pointer-free traversal, filter editing, and 200 percent zoom were not attended, so this is not a keyboard pass.

screen-reader.notes: The operator could not complete VoiceOver. DOM text exposed status, current written pitch, staff, and bounded history, but DOM or WebDriver inspection does not establish narration or prove raw SVG is suppressed. Operator step: enable VoiceOver and navigate idle, permission, listening, silence, note, denial, device-loss, and recovery states without a pointer.

privacy-network.notes: The operator could not attend Safari Web Inspector Network from a cleared log through Start, active capture, preference changes, and Stop. Operator step: clear Network before Start, exercise filters, monitor, calibration, and Stop, and fail on any audio, detection, preference, analytics, telemetry, or third-party executable request.

sustained.notes: Active capture lasted less than 30 minutes and the monitor was enabled only briefly. Battery moved from 80 to 61 percent while discharging during combined development and browser work; this is not attributable battery evidence. No active-memory, dropped-update, frozen-display, or sustained thermal claim is made.
